---
title: AI War Cloud
subtitle: AI decision making from battlefield to desktop
# date: Aug 1 2025
keywords: artificial intelligence, artistic research, automated decision making, autonomous weapons, critical AI, defense, generative AI, international security, machine learning, military, surveillance, technoimperialism, tech industry, warfare
---
**AI War Cloud is an interactive database and tangible interactive installation connecting and explaining the current techno-imperial boomerang that is perpetuated by machine learning.** 

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | column: page
# | label: fig-graph
## | fig-cap: "AI War Cloud Database connects AI decision support systems and machine learning tasks as they are used across military and commercial industries."
## | scroll: 800px
## | classes: cell

import pandas as pd
from pandas import Series, DataFrame
import networkx as nx
import gravis as gv
from pprint import pprint

Sheets = pd.ExcelFile("./data/aiwc.ods")

# create graph with edges from csv import
Edges_Deployed = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "E_isDeployedBy")
Edges_Developed = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "E_isDevelopedBy")
Edges_Place = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "E_place")
Edges_Connection = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "E_connection")
Edges_People = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "E_people")
all_edges = [Edges_Deployed, Edges_Developed, Edges_Place, Edges_Connection, Edges_People]
Edges = pd.concat(all_edges)

g = nx.from_pandas_edgelist(Edges, 
                            source='source', 
                            target='target', 
                            edge_attr=['label'], 
                            create_using=nx.Graph(), #DiGraph
                            edge_key=False)

# print(g.edges())


# assign data to nodes (stakeholders)
Stakeholders = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "N_Stakeholders") #, header=0, index_col=0
Stakeholders = Stakeholders.T # transforms from long to wide table
Stakeholders.index_col = 'id'

StakeDict = Stakeholders.to_dict()
# pprint((StakeDict))

for d in StakeDict:
    g.nodes[StakeDict[d]['id']].update(StakeDict[d]) 

# assign data to nodes (systems)
Systems = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "N_Systems")
Systems = Systems.T # transforms from long to wide table
Systems.index_col = 'id'

SysDict = Systems.to_dict()

for d in SysDict:
    g.nodes[SysDict[d]['id']].update(SysDict[d]) 

# assign data to nodes (civic)
Civic = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "N_Civic")
Civic = Civic.T # transforms from long to wide table
Civic.index_col = 'id'

CivDict = Civic.to_dict()

for d in CivDict:
    g.nodes[CivDict[d]['id']].update(CivDict[d])

# assign data to nodes (history)
Hist = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "N_Historical")
Hist = Hist.T # transforms from long to wide table
Hist.index_col = 'id'

HistDict = Hist.to_dict()

for d in HistDict:
    g.nodes[HistDict[d]['id']].update(HistDict[d])

# assign data to nodes (people)
People = pd.read_excel(Sheets, sheet_name = "N_People")
Peep = People.T # transforms from long to wide table
Peep.index_col = 'id'

PeepDict = Peep.to_dict()

for d in Peep:
    g.nodes[PeepDict[d]['id']].update(PeepDict[d])

### test nodes
# print(g.nodes.data())
# print(g.nodes['Lavender']['nounKey'])

# visual settings
gg = g.graph
gg['edge_opacity'] = 0.25
gg['node_opacity'] = 0 #.75
# gg['node_shape'] = 'rectangle'
# gg['node_border_size'] = 0
# gg['edge_label_size'] = 5
gg['node_label_size'] = 9
# gg['node_color'] = 'gray'
# gg['border_size'] = 0
# gg['node_hover'] = '$name'
gg['node_click'] = 'Details: $hover'

# centrality = nx.algorithms.degree_centrality(g)
# nx.set_node_attributes(g, centrality, 'size')

# communities = nx.algorithms.community.louvain_communities(g)
# communities = nx.algorithms.community.greedy_modularity_communities(g)
# colors = ['#51DBD9', '#5186DB', '#51B2DB', '#51DBAB', '#515ADB', '#009ADB']
# for community, color in zip(communities, colors):
#   for node in community:
#     g.nodes[node]['color'] = color #add edges to this too

gv.three(g, 
        graph_height=800,
        zoom_factor=-1,
        show_details=False,
        show_details_toggle_button=True,
        show_node_label=True, 
        node_label_data_source='name',
        # node_size_data_source='year',
        node_size_factor = 2,
        # node_click='details',
        # use_node_size_normalization=True,
        # node_size_normalization_max=20,
        # show_edge_label=False,
        # edge_size_data_source='size',
        # edge_size_normalization_max=3,
        # edge_size_normalization_min=0.5,
        # edge_label_data_source='label',
        edge_curvature=0,
        node_hover_neighborhood=True,
        node_hover_tooltip=True,
        edge_hover_tooltip=True,
        # use_collision_force=True,
        many_body_force_strength=-1100,
        many_body_force_theta=0.5,
        node_drag_fix=True,
        show_node_image=True,
        node_image_size_factor=1.75,
        show_node_label_border=False,
        show_edge_label_border=False,
        # node_label_rotation=0,
        node_label_font='IBM Plex Mono',
        use_x_positioning_force = True,
        x_positioning_force_strength = 0.04,
        use_y_positioning_force = True,
        y_positioning_force_strength = 0.07,
        use_z_positioning_force = True,
        z_positioning_force_strength = 0.11,
        # use_centering_force = False,
)


```


Bibliography: [References](about.html#full-bibliography)

Code: [GitLab](https://gitlab.com/sarahciston/aiwar)

[Credits and Acknowledgments](about.html#credits)
